originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__pid_output_699' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'pid_output' is never read
presenceCondition:
(let ((a!1 (and (or (and MARLIN_H (not PIDTEMP))
                    (and MARLIN_H PIDTEMP PID_DEBUG))
                (and MARLIN_H (not PIDTEMP))))
      (a!2 (and (or (and (not MARLIN_H) (not PIDTEMP))
                    (and (not MARLIN_H) PIDTEMP PID_DEBUG))
                (and (not MARLIN_H) (not PIDTEMP)))))
  (or a!1 a!2))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  false)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  true)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 8c4377dd635.c: Dead store of `pid_output` — initialized to 1 at L11, possibly reassigned to 0 at L19 (when !PIDTEMP), but never read in any path (no code uses pid_output after assignment). Not marked ERROR — new bug. PC simplifies to !PIDTEMP (MARLIN_H branches factor out due to AND structure). PC is under-constrained: the bug also exists when PIDTEMP=T (pid_output=1 at L11 is dead since L19 doesn't execute and pid_output is never used). Real condition: all compilable configs (true, minus MARLIN_H=T AND PIDTEMP=T AND PID_DEBUG=T which is non-compilable).
